questions.text: Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate option. When we went to cinema, the film _.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: “When we went” indicates that the particular action was completed in the past. Hence the statement is in past perfect tense, implying the usage of ‘had’ with the 2nd form of the verb. So, the grammatically correct is, When we went to the cinema, the film had already started.

questions.explanation: The other options are wrong as 'when we went' indicates the use of the past tense. The correct answer is B) had already started.

questions.text: Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate option. The soup _ good.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: “The soup” being singular requires a singular verb in accordance with the subject verb agreement, which out of all these options is “tastes”. So, the correct sentence will be, The soup tastes good.

questions.explanation: "Tastes" in option B is the singular form of the verb and is in subject verb agreement. Thus, this is correct.

questions.text: Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate option. She _ unconscious since 4 o’clock.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: “since four o’clock” indicates that the sentence is in present perfect tense, implying the use of has been in the sentence. So, the correct sentence will be, She has been unconscious since four o’clock.

questions.explanation: The other options are wrong as 'since four o'clock' indicates the use of present perfect tense and not the simple present or simple past tenses.

questions.text: Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate option. We will stay here _ Monday.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: Till is used to specify the time a specific event ends. Since is used to mention the start of an event from the past. Still indicates the continuity of an already happening action. Until is usually used to add condition. Hence, till is used here. So, the correct sentence will be; She will stay here Till Monday.

questions.explanation: Till is correct as it means up to. The other options are wrong as by, at, and to indicate usage, a specific location and a direction respectively.

questions.text: The word curiosity means:
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: Cruel; causing pain or suffering.
Curiosity means an eager desire to learn and is a synonym of keen. Keen; having or showing eagerness or enthusiasm.
Angry; feeling or showing strong annoyance, displeasure, or hostility
Killing; an act of causing death, especially deliberately

questions.explanation: Curiosity refers to being marked by a desire to investigate and learn, or in simpler words being eager or keen.

questions.text: Choose the correct spelling:
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: The correct word is entrepreneur meaning a person who sets up a business or businesses, taking on financial risks in the hope of profit.

questions.explanation: The correct spelling is entrepreneur. It refers to a person who sets up a business or businesses, taking on financial risks in the hope of profit.

questions.text: DRAWBACK, this word is a combination of:
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: Drawback is composed of two words; Draw and Back. Draw is a verb which means to produce an image of (someone or something) by making lines and marks on paper while Back is an adverb which is used to describe doing the same action to someone in return.

questions.explanation: Draw is a verb, while back is an adverb.

questions.text: “Which is the next up train?” What does "up" represent here?
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: In the context of "Which is the next up train?", "up" is an adjective. It modifies the noun "train" and indicates the direction of travel.In this specific case, "up" is an adjective used to describe the type of train, distinguishing it from a "down" train.

questions.explanation: In the context of "Which is the next up train?", "up" is an adjective. It modifies the noun "train" and indicates the direction of travel.In this specific case, "up" is an adjective used to describe the type of train, distinguishing it from a "down" train.

questions.text: I wish I _ the answer.
questions.subject: English
questions.explanation: Knew here means the past tense.

questions.text: What does the following word mean? Analogue
questions.subject: English
questions.explanation: Analogue: something that is similar or comparable to something else either in general or in some specific detail.

questions.text: What does the following word mean? Pulsating
questions.subject: English
questions.explanation: Pulsating: characterized by a strong regular rhythm; throbbing.

questions.text: The person in charge of a school is called a _.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: Principal; the head of a school, college, or other educational institution
Principle; a fundamental source or basis of something.
Head; a person in charge of something
Staff; all the people employed by a particular organization.

questions.explanation: A principle is a rule, a law, a guideline, or a fact. A principal is the headmaster of a school or a person who's in charge of certain things in a company.

questions.text: Break a leg is commonly used to:
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: Break a leg is an English idiom used to wish good luck to someone, especially before a performance. Its usage in a sentence is as follows Danny's family told him to “break a leg” right before he went up on stage.

questions.explanation: Break a leg is used for wishing someone good luck, especially before a performance.

questions.text: Bite the bullet is commonly used to:
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: Bite the bullet is an English idiom used to refer to getting over something over with because it is inevitable. An example of its use in a sentence is as follows; "Decisions have to be taken and as director you have got to bite the bullet"

questions.explanation: Bite the bullet is to get something over with because it is inevitable.

questions.text: If fertilization occurs, the young embryo is implanted into the:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The embryo implants into the innermost wall of the uterus i.e., the endometrium.

questions.explanation: The endometrium is the inner layer that lines the uterus. It is made up of glandular cells that make secretions and the place where young embryos are implanted. The myometrium is the middle and thickest layer of the uterus wall. It is made up mostly of smooth muscle. The perimetrium is the outer serous layer of the uterus.

questions.text: Sperms are produced in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The urethra is a duct that carries urine and semen out of the body but is not involved in the production of sperm.
"Sperm dust" is not a recognized term or structure. It does not correspond to any anatomical or physiological component in the male reproductive system.
The pancreas is an organ involved in the digestive system and secretes enzymes and hormones, but it does not produce sperm.
The testes are the male reproductive organs responsible for producing sperm through a process called spermatogenesis.

questions.explanation: Explanation for this question will be added soon.

questions.text: Hormones called gonadotropins are released by which gland?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Gonadotrophic hormones stimulate the gonads to secrete their respective hormones e.g., testosterone and estrogen. These gonadotrophic hormones are secreted by pituitary gland and include LH, FSH, etc.

questions.explanation: Gonadotropins are released by the pituitary gland. Examples include LH, FSH etc.

questions.text: Which of the following is found at the end of long bones, in the nose, larynx, and trachea?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Elastic cartilage is found in external ear pinna and epiglottis.
Fibrous cartilage is found in vertebral discs and ligaments.
Perichondrium is a dense layer of fibrous connective tissue that covers the surface of most of the cartilage in the body.
Hyaline cartilage is most abundant type of cartilage found in our body. It is present in the nose, at the end of long bones, ears, smaller respiratory tubes, larynx and trachea.

questions.explanation: Hyaline cartilage is the most abundant type of cartilage and is found in the nose, ears, trachea, larynx, and smaller respiratory tubes. Fibrocartilage is found in vertebral discs and ligaments. Elastic cartilage is found in the external ear and epiglottis.

questions.text: A muscle with characters like a spontaneous contraction, one nucleus per cell, and no control over contraction is most likely:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Refer to the following table from Punjab Board Textbook.

questions.text: “Ball and socket” joint is present at:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: The "ball" is the top, rounded part of the humerus, and the "socket" is the bowl-shaped part of the scapula,into which the ball fits. Elbow and knee joints are hinge joints. Wrist joint is the Gliding joint.

questions.text: Which human body function is NOT controlled through negative feedback mechanism?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Oxytocin stimulates the muscle contractions that push the baby through the birth canal. The release of oxytocin result in stronger or augmented contractions during labor. Thus Labour contraction is an example of positive feedback.

questions.text: Gray matter of spinal cord does not contain:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Gray matter, the inner matter of the spinal cord does not contain cell bodies of sensory neurons.

questions.text: In term of enzyme action, maximum temperature refers to a temperature at which?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Maximum temperature refers to a temperature at which enzymes start to breakdown and denature.

questions.text: In enzymatic activity, the term “feedback inhibition” is used when:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Enzyme is inhibited by the product being produced
Enzyme works normally for regulatory mechanism
Enzyme remains unaffected by product concentration

questions.explanation: Feedback regulation of an enzyme occurs when a product of the reaction binds to an allosteric site on the enzyme and affects its catalytic activity. This results in an reversible inhibition of enzyme.

questions.text: The chemical which regulates the enzymatic activity is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Enzyme activity can be regulated by other molecules that increase or decrease the rate of reaction/ activity. Molecules that increase the enzyme activity are called “activators” and molecules which decrease the enzyme activity are called “inhibitors”.

questions.explanation: Enzymes can be regulated by other molecules that either increase or reduce their activity. Molecules that increase the activity of an enzyme are called activators, while molecules that decrease the activity of an enzyme are called inhibitors.

questions.text: At temperature higher than optimum, enzymatic activity decreases because of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Enzymes are made up of proteins. Proteins are temperature sensitive. At temperature higher than optimal temperature the shape of active site of enzyme is lost. This is termed as denaturation.

questions.explanation: Enzymes are protein in nature. At a temperature higher than optimum temperature, the 3D shape of active site denatures and substrate molecules can no longer fit into it. This is termed as denaturation of enzymes.

questions.text: Which of the following ideas was not part of Charles Darwin’s theory of evolution by natural selection?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Organisms produce more off springs than the environment can support
options.explanation: This idea is a fundamental aspect of Darwin's theory. Darwin observed that populations have the potential for exponential growth, but resources in the environment are limited. As a result, there is competition for resources, and not all offspring can survive and reproduce.

options.text: Variation between individuals arises by gene mutation
options.explanation: Charles Darwin's theory of evolution by natural selection included the idea that variation exists among individuals within a population. However, the specific idea that this variation arises primarily through gene mutation was not part of Darwin's original theory.

options.text: Only those individuals best adapted by environment survive and reproduce
options.explanation: This idea is a central concept in Darwin's theory of natural selection. Darwin proposed that individuals with traits that provide them with advantages in their environment are more likely to survive and reproduce, passing on their advantageous traits to future generations.

This idea is also an important part of Darwin's theory. Darwin recognized that resources necessary for survival and reproduction, such as food, water, and shelter, are limited in nature. As a result, individuals within a population must compete with each other for access to these resources.

questions.explanation: The idea that was not part of Charles Darwin's theory of evolution by natural selection is Option B: Variation between individuals arises by gene mutation. While Darwin acknowledged the existence of variation, he did not have a detailed understanding of the genetic mechanisms behind it, such as gene mutations.

questions.text: Evolution of kiwi, food of modern horse, webbed feet of duck, and loss of teeth in whale were all explained in detail by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Lamark presented Lamarkism. A theory of evolution based on the principal that evolution is based on principal changes in an organism during their lifetime. Above mentioned examples were explained by him as a proof of this theory.

questions.explanation: Lamarckism is a theory of evolution based on the principle that physical changes in organisms during their lifetime. Examples include evolution of kiwi, foot of modern horse, webbed feet of duck, and loss of teeth in whale.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT TRUE about interferons?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Interleukins (IFNs) are a broad class of cytokines that are a part of our Second Line of Defense. They activate natural killer cells, activate immune cells and alert other body cells about the presence of pathogenic agents. They are named as interferons because they “interfere” with the virus’s ability to multiply.

questions.explanation: Interferons (IFNs) are a broad class of cytokines that are part of your natural defenses. They tell your immune system that germs or cancer cells are in your body. And they trigger killer immune cells to fight those invaders. Interferons got their name because they "interfere" with viruses and keep them from multiplying.

questions.text: Intrinsic factor secreted by parietal cells of stomach mucosa helps in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Intrinsic factor is secreted by the parietal cells. It plays a crucial role is absorption of vitamins and other micronutrients. Protein digestion is performed in stomach by pepsin. Carbohydrates are not broken down in stomach. Bowel movement is excretion of stool outside the body.

questions.explanation: Intrinsic factor plays a crucial role in the transportation and absorption of the vital micronutrient vitamin B12.

questions.text: Major volume of gastric juices is produced in stomach by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Presence of protein food in stomach
options.explanation: All factors mentioned in the options above induce the secretion of gastric juice. However, greatest volume of it secreted when protein food is present inside the stomach.

questions.explanation: Presence of proteins in the stomach activates parietal cells to secrete gastric juice.

questions.text: Which one is NOT a metabolic role of liver?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Metabolic roles of liver include glycogenolysis (conversion of glycogen to glucose), gluconeogenesis (formation of glucose from sources other than carbohydrates) and Deamination (removal of amino group from proteins). Immunoglobin synthesis is performed by B-Lymphocytes.

questions.explanation: Metabolic role of liver includes Glycogenolysis (conversion of glycogen to glucose), Gluconeogenesis(formation of glucose from noncarbohydrate resources), and Deamination (removal of amine group from protein) while Immunoglobulin synthesis is the function of B-Lymphocytes.

questions.text: The enzyme which is NOT produced by the pancreas during digestion is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Pancreatic juice contains Lipase, Trypsin and Amylase. It does not contain Pepsin. Pepsin is secreted by walls of stomach.

questions.explanation: Pancreatic juice contains Lipase, Trypsin, and Amylase. Thus it does not contain Pepsin and Option C is the correct answer.

questions.text: The heart chamber from where the aorta originates is the:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Aorta is the largest blood vessel in the body. It originates from the left ventricle of the heart.

questions.explanation: The aorta is the largest vessel within the human body. It originates from the left ventricle of the heart anterior to the pulmonary artery before arching posteriorly and descending along the posterior mediastinum.

questions.text: Lymphatic system contains all of the organs/structures except:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The five structures of the lymphatic system are bone marrow, spleen, thymus, Lymph nodes and lymphatic vessels. Lungs are not the part of lymphatic system rather they are the part of the respiratory system.

questions.explanation: The four organs of the lymphatic system are bone marrow, spleen, thymus, lymph nodes, and lymphatic vessels. Thus lungs are not part of it and option D is the correct answer.

questions.text: The smaller (40S) of ribosome combines with larger subunit (60S) in the presence of Mg2+ ion to form _ particle.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The S written alongside the numerical entity stands of Svedberg Units. It represents the rate of sedimentation during centrifugation. These can not be added like normal integers. In eukaryotes 60S and 40S subunits combine to form 80S ribosome. In prokaryotes 50S and 30S subunits combine to form 70S ribosome.

questions.explanation: The S in the ribosomal subunits stands for svedberg units. It represents the different sedimentation rates of the ribosomes during centrifugation.The 60s and 40s ribosomal subunits join to form an 80S ribosome found in eukaryotes.

questions.text: The natural bacterial flora is beneficial to humans because they:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Natural bacterial flora are the bacterial species that live inside the human body and are beneficial for it. This flora prevents the colonization by pathogens by competing for attachment and nutrients

questions.explanation: Bacterial flora is beneficial because it prevents colonisation by pathogens by competing for attachment & nutrients.

questions.text: In bacteria, the resistance against heat is provided by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Concentrated deposits of certain substances presented/located in the cytoplasm of certain bacteria are known as granules or inclusion bodies.

A microbial cyst is a resting or dormant stage of a microorganism, usually a bacterium or a protist or rarely an invertebrate animal, that helps the organism to survive in unfavorable environmental conditions such as nutrient deprivation or presence of chemicals.

Spores are thick, dormant structures that provide resistance or protection against harsh and adverse conditions. They are formed by bacteria to protect themselves from adverse environmental conditions such as heat, UV rays, nutrient deprivation, presence of toxic chemicals etc.
Plasmids are structures used for conjugation (sexual reproduction in bacteria)

questions.explanation: Spores are thick, dormant, and tough structures produced by some bacteria that provide resistance or protection against harsh or adverse conditions.They are formed by the bacteria to protect themselves from adverse environmental conditions such as heat, UV rays, nutrient deprivation, presence of toxic chemicals, etc.

questions.text: These hormones perform antagonistic function EXCEPT:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Somatostatin inhibits the release of growth hormones.
Calcitonin decreases blood Ca+2 levels whereas parathormone increases blood Ca+2 levels.
The correct option is C as both adrenaline and noradrenaline are involved in the fight or flight response in times of emergency, hence, they are not antagonistic.
Insulin decreases blood glucose level and glucagon increases blood glucose level.

questions.explanation: A is incorrect because somatostatin inhibits the release of growth hormone from the pituitary gland. B is incorrect because calcitonin decreases blood Ca2+ levels while parathormone increases blood calcium levels. D is incorrect because insulin decreased blood glucose concentration and glucagon raises blood glucose concentration. Adrenaline and noradrenaline are both involved in the fight or flight response during times of emergency. Hence, they are not antagonistic.

questions.text: Virus recognize host through:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The protein coat of a virus contains receptors. The host-virus recognition and attachment are via these receptors. The DNA or the genetic material enters the host cell afterwards. The envelope itself is derived from the host.

questions.explanation: A virus’ protein coat contains receptors. The virus recognizes and binds to a host cell via a receptor. The virus or its genetic material then enters the host cell. The envelope itself is derived from the host.

questions.text: Cell surface markers are
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Cell surface markers are actually glycoproteins in nature. They may include receptors that help in attachment of antibodies or help in responding and sensing the environment around them. Cell surface markers are actually glycoproteins in nature. They may include receptors that help in attachment of antibodies or help in responding and sensing the environment around them. ell surface markers are primarily glycoproteins. Glycoproteins are proteins that have attached carbohydrate chains or groups. These carbohydrate chains on glycoproteins act as cell surface markers or antigens, allowing cells to recognize and communicate with each other. Lipoproteins, glycolipids, and sphingolipids are also present in cell membranes and have various functions, but they are not specifically referred to as cell surface markers.e markers or antigens, allowing cells to recognize and communicate with each other. Lipoproteins, glycolipids, and sphingolipids are also present in cell membranes and have various functions, but they are not specifically referred to as cell surface markers.

questions.explanation: Cell-surface markers are glycoproteins that are able to respond to or sense the environment around the cell and contain receptors that help in detection by an antibody.

questions.text: Porins are present in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Porins are special proteins that allow the passage of materials across the membrane. These are present in outer membrane of chloroplast and mitochondria.

questions.explanation: Porins are present in the outer membrane of mitochondria and the outer chloroplast membrane.

questions.text: The role of parietal cells of mucosa is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Parietal or oxyntic cells are mainly responsible for the secretion of HCl. This HCl aids in digestion of food, control of bacteria and activation of enzymes.

questions.explanation: Parietal cells are responsible for HCl secretion, which aids in the digestion of food, absorption of minerals, and control of harmful bacteria.

questions.text: Structure present in both prokaryotes and eukaryotes:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: 80S ribosome is the feature of eukaryotic cells. Whereas prokaryotic cells are characterized as 70S.
Cell wall is not present in all but some prokaryotes and eukaryotes.
Cell membrane is the coorect option and is a structure common in all life cells.
Golgi apparatus is a membrane bound organelle specific to eukaryotes.

questions.explanation: Ribosomes are used by the cells for the translation of mRNA to produce proteins. Eukaryotic cells typically have 80s ribosomes and 70s ribosomes (in mitochondria and plastids), whereas prokaryotic cells have 70s ribosomes. Not all eukaryotic cells have cell wall, hence the correct option is C since cell membrane is present in both types of cells.

questions.text: Which organelle has hydrolytic enzymes?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: ER are used for transport and many other functions.
Ribosomes synthesis proteins.
Lysosomes are also referred to as the dustbin of the cell. They are membrane bound organelles that contain hydrolytic enzymes for the breakdown of the cell wastes.
Cell wall covers the whole cell.

questions.explanation: Lysosomes are also refered as the dustbin of the cell. They contain hydrolytic enzymes for the breakdown of the cell wastes.

questions.text: Number and sequence of amino acids is determined by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Primary structure, is simply the sequence of amino acids in a polypeptide chain.
Secondary structure is conformation in to helical or sheet like structure.
Tertiary structure is the formation of a globular structure
Quaternary structure is the adhesion of multiple globular structures.

questions.explanation: Primary structure, is simply the sequence of amino acids in a polypeptide chain.

questions.text: Water is a solvent due to:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Due to its polarity and ability to form hydrogen bonds water is considered as the universal solvent.

questions.explanation: Water is a good solvent because of its polarity and ability to form hydrogen bonds.

questions.text: Carbohydrates contain:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Carbohydrates, as the name suggests, contains the elements carbon, hydrogen and oxygen. They are the hydrates of Carbon, with general formula Cn(H2O)n. A typical example would be glucose.

questions.explanation: Carbohydrates, as the name suggests, contains the elements carbon , hydrogen and oxygen. A typical example would be glucose.

questions.text: Most of RNA is present in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The nucleolus is the site of the highest RNA synthesis rate in cell nuclei giving rise to more than 60% of the entire RNA pool.

questions.explanation: The nucleolus is the site of the highest RNA synthesis rate in cell nuclei giving rise to more than 60% of the entire RNA pool.

questions.text: Turgidity in plants is maintained by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The membrane that bounds vacuole, is called tonoplast which contains a number of active transport systems that pump ions into the vacuole or vacuolar compartments despite the higher concentration than that of the extracellular fluid. Because of the higher ionic concentration, water enters the vacuole and hence provides turgidity, mechanical support, to soft tissues of plant. This vacuole in plants is centrally placed and pushes nucleus to the side.

questions.explanation: "The membrane that bounds vacuole, is called tonoplast which contains a number of active transport systems that pump ions into the vacuole or vacuolar compartments despite the higher concentration than that of the extracellular fluid. Because of the higher ionic concentration, water enters the vacuole and hence provides turgidity, mechanical support, to soft tissues of plant." (PTB)

questions.text: Chlorophyll pigments are present in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Chloroplast is a plastid specialized for the process of photosynthesis. It is green in color due to the presence of the photosynthesizing pigment i.e., chlorophyll.

questions.explanation: Greenish pigment carrier for photosynthesis present in the chlorophyll in the cell.

questions.text: Xanthophylls are which pigment?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Xanthophylls (originally phylloxanthins) are yellow pigments that occur widely in nature and form one of two major divisions of the carotenoid group.

questions.explanation: Xanthophylls are accessory pigments in the thylakoid membrane of chloroplast in plant cells. They belong to the group carotenoids.

questions.text: Pathway from receptor to effector in which conscious part of brain is not involved is the:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A reflex, is an involuntary and nearly instantaneous movement in response to a stimulus. A reflex arc is a neural pathway that controls a reflex. In vertebrates, most sensory neurons do not pass directly into the brain, but synapse in the spinal cord. This allows for faster reflex actions to occur by activating spinal motor neurons without the delay of routing signals through the brain. A signal however is indeed sent to the brain to inform it of what happened.
Sensory pathways consist of the chain of neurons, from receptor organ to cerebral cortex, that are responsible for the perception of sensations.
Motor pathways carry signals from the brain to skeletal muscle and smooth muscle such as those contained in glands.

The somatosensory tracts (also referred to as the somatosensory system or somatosensory pathways) process information about somatic sensations such as pain, temperature, touch, position, and vibration. This information is received through receptors inside or at the surface of the body.

questions.explanation: A reflex, is an involuntary and nearly instantaneous movement in response to a stimulus.A reflex arc is a neural pathway that controls a reflex. In vertebrates, most sensory neurons do not pass directly into the brain, but synapse in the spinal cord. This allows for faster reflex actions to occur by activating spinal motor neurons without the delay of routing signals through the brain.

questions.text: Which of the following organelles has its own DNA?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Mitochondria and chloroplast are self-replicating organelles which contain their own DNA, enzymes and 70S ribosomes and synthesizes their own proteins

questions.explanation: It is a self replicating organelle which contains its own DNA and synthesises its own proteins.

questions.text: Which organelle performs a role in cell wall formation?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Ribosomes are cellular structures responsible for protein synthesis. They are not directly involved in cell wall formation.

The nuclear membrane (also known as the nuclear envelope) surrounds the cell's nucleus and controls the movement of substances in and out of the nucleus. It is not directly involved in cell wall formation.

Plasmids are small, circular pieces of DNA that are found in some types of cells, such as bacteria. They can carry genes that may provide specific traits to the cell, but they are not directly involved in the formation of cell walls in plant cells.

The Golgi apparatus and plasma membranes are the two main sites where constituents of cell wall are synthesized. At the time of cell wall formation, vesicles from the Golgi apparatus arrange themselves along the center of cell and later fuse to form the cell wall.

questions.explanation: The Golgi and plasma membranes are the two main sites where constituents of cell wall are synthesized.

questions.text: Steroids synthesis occurs in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The SER plays many vital roles in the cell, one of which includes synthesis of lipids. Steroids and some hormones being lipid in nature are synthesized by the SER.

questions.explanation: SER is responsible for the synthesis of hormones and steroids. It also plays a major role in detoxication.

questions.text: The master gland in the human body is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The pituitary gland is sometimes called the "master" gland of the endocrine system because it controls the secretions of many of the other endocrine glands by the secretion of trophic hormones.

Your thyroid is a small, butterfly-shaped gland located at the front of your neck under your skin. It's a part of your endocrine system and controls many of your body's important functions by producing and releasing (secreting) certain hormones.

The parathyroid glands lie just behind the thyroid glands in the neck. The parathyroid glands (light pink) produce parathyroid hormone, which increases levels of calcium in the blood. The parathyroid glands are small pea-sized glands located in the neck just behind the butterfly-shaped thyroid gland.
Glandular organ located on the chest. The mammary gland is made up of connective tissue, fat, and tissue that contains the glands that can make milk. Also called breast.

questions.explanation: The pituitary gland is sometimes called the "master" gland of the endocrine system because it controls the functions of many of the other endocrine glands.

questions.text: End product of light reaction of photosynthesis is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Light reaction is also known as the energy conversion phase, as in this phase light energy is absorbed by chlorophyll molecules and converted into chemical energy via the synthesis of energy rich molecules i.e. ATP and NADPH.

questions.explanation: ATP and NADPH are synthesized in the light reactions of photosynthesis, which are then used in the Calvin cycle to produce sugars.

questions.text: Definition of hydrolysis:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Combining of molecules due to reaction with hydrogen
Combining of molecules due to reaction with water
Breaking of molecules due to reaction with water
options.explanation: The word hydrolysis is composed of two words, “hydro” meaning “water” and “lysis” meaning “breakdown”. Hence, hydrolysis is the breakdown of a substance in presence of water.

options.text: Breaking of molecules due to reaction with hydrogen

questions.explanation: The 'lysis' in hydrolysis means splitting. It is the splitting of molecule using water.

questions.text: Which of following is a structural protein?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Pepsinogen is a protein digesting enzyme found in the stomach making it a functional protein.
Casein and enzymes are also functional proteins.
Casein and enzymes are also functional proteins.
Collagen is the main structural protein in the extracellular matrix found in the body's various connective tissues.

questions.explanation: Collagen is the main structural protein in the extracellular matrix found in the body's various connective tissues.

questions.text: Transmission of impulse is done by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: SER is responsible for hormone and steroids synthesis, detoxification, impulse transmission. The extensive smooth ER network within neurons modulates the excitation and transmission of nerve impulses from one cell to another, and even allows local changes to Ca2+ concentration (due to its ability to store Ca2+ ions).

questions.explanation: SER is responsible for 1. Hormone and steroids synthesis 2. Detoxification 3. Impulse transmission.

questions.text: The pH of human blood is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Blood is normally slightly basic, with a normal pH range of about 7.35 to 7.45. Usually, the human body maintains the pH of blood close to 7.40.

questions.explanation: Blood is normally slightly basic, with a normal pH range of about 7.35 to 7.45. Usually the body maintains the pH of blood close to 7.40.

questions.text: In an X-linked recessive disease, a affected woman married to a normal man, the percentage of affected daughters is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: One X chromosome always comes from the female and one X (in case of female) or Y (in case of male) comes from the man. If the man is normal then the disease will not express in the daughter, because it is X-linked recessive and to affect daughters (XX) both the parents must have the gene.

questions.explanation: X chromosome comes from the female and X or Y comes from the man. if the man is normal then the disease will not express in the daughter.

questions.text: The number of ATP used to produce 1 glucose in Calvin cycle is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Calvin cycle when once completed produces a 3-carbon compound (G3P) and requires 3 CO2, 9 ATP and 6 NADPH. So, to make glucose (a 6-carbon compound), it would take two turns of the cycle, or 6 CO2, 18 ATP, and 12 NADPH, to produce one molecule of glucose.

questions.explanation: It would take six turns of the cycle, or 6 CO2 ​start text, 18 ATP, and 12 NADPH, to produce one molecule of glucose.

questions.text: Which part of virus has glycoproteins?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The viral envelope is often covered with spikes made of glycoproteins that help them to recognize the host cell.

questions.explanation: The viral envelope is often covered with glycoprotein spikes that help them to recognize the host cell.

questions.text: Which hexose is most important from a biological point of view?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Glucose is the main source of energy for cellular respiration.

questions.explanation: Glucose is the main source of energy.

questions.text: Chromosomes that are same in male and female are called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Humans have twenty-three pairs of chromosomes. Twenty-two of these pairs, called autosomes, look the same in both males and females. One pair, called the sex chromosomes is different is both, in males it is XY and in females the pair is XX.

questions.explanation: Twenty-two of these pairs, called autosomes, look the same in both males and females.

questions.text: In the light reaction, water is used in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: In light reaction water is broken down by highly oxidizing chlorophyll molecule in presence of sunlight (photolysis). This photolysis of water provides with electrons (taken up by chlorophyll), hydrogen ions and oxygen.

questions.explanation: It provides protons and electrons for further process of photosynthesis.

questions.text: Light reaction takes place in which part of the chloroplast?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The light reaction takes place in the thylakoid membranes. In the light reaction, the light is absorbed by photosystems found in the thylakoid and energy is used to drive electrons derived from water to generate NADPH and to drive protons across the membrane, for the generation of ATP.

questions.explanation: The light reaction takes place in the thylakoid membranes. In the light reaction, the light is absorbed and energy is used to drive electrons from water to generate NADPH and to drive protons across the membrane.

questions.text: Which of the following attaches bones to muscles?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Ligaments attach bones to bones.
A tendon is a fibrous connective tissue which attaches bone to muscle.

questions.explanation: A tendon is a fibrous connective tissue which attach bones to muscles.

questions.text: The outer most living boundary of the cell is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The plasma membrane of all cells represents the universal boundary between the cell and its surroundings. It is the outermost insulation of the animal cells as it is elastic and the property of semipermeable membrane helps control what enters and exits the cell. It is the final living boundary of cell, cell wall that is present outside the cell membrane in some cells is a non-living component.

questions.explanation: The plasma membrane of all cells represents the universal boundary between the cell and its surroundings. It is the outermost insulation of the animal cells as it is elastic and the property of semipermeable membrane helps control what enters and exits the cell. Students can get confused with cell wall but it should be remembered that cell wall is non-living.

questions.text: The sensation of pain is produced by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The pain receptors are nociceptors. They are known to exist in muscle, joints, and skin.

questions.explanation: The pain receptors are nociceptors. They are known to exist in muscle, joints, and skin.

questions.text: The resting membrane potential is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The resting membrane potential of a neuron is the electrical potential inside the cell relative to the adjacent extracellular space when no impulse is passing i.e. the neuron is at rest. It has a value of -70 mV.

questions.explanation: The resting membrane potential of a neuron is the electrical potential inside the cell relative to the adjacent extracellular space.

questions.text: Which neurotransmitter is present in the periperal nervous system?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Acetylcholine is a type of chemical messenger, or neurotransmitter, that plays a vital role in the central and peripheral nervous system. It is important for muscle control, autonomic body functions, and in learning, memory, and attention.
Dopamine and Serotonin are neurotransmitters of the CNS and epinephrine is a hormone secreted in emergency situtations.
Dopamine and Serotonin are neurotransmitters of the CNS and epinephrine is a hormone secreted in emergency situtations.

questions.explanation: Acetylcholine is a type of chemical messenger, or neurotransmitter, that plays a vital role in the central and peripheral nervous system. It is important for muscle control, autonomic body functions, and in learning, memory, and attention.

questions.text: Most ideal gas in the following is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The real gas that acts most like an ideal gas is helium. Because it exists in a single atom and its Van Der Waal’s forces are very low due to its small atomic size.

questions.explanation: The real gas that acts most like an ideal gas is helium. Because it exists in a single atom and its vander waals forces are very low.

questions.text: Two ice blocks stick together due to:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: When two ice cubes are pressed over each other, they unite to form one cube, Hydrogen bonding is responsible for this process, when pushed together their molecules form these intermolecular forces between them.

options.text: All of the above options are correct

questions.explanation: When two ice cubes are pressed over each other, they unite to form one cube, Hydrogen bonding is responsible for it. Hence option C is correct.

questions.text: Unit of rate and rate constant are same for:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: In the zero-order reaction, the rate is independent of the concentration of reactants and hence, the units of rate and rate constant become equal that is mol.dm-3s-1.

questions.explanation: In the zero-order reaction, the rate is independent of the concentration of reactants and hence, the units of rate and rate constant become equal which is mol/L/time. So, the correct answer is option D.

questions.text: Reduction occurs at:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Oxidation always occurs at the anode and reduction always occurs at the cathode. Since the reaction at the anode is the source of electrons for the current, the anode is the negative terminal and cathode is the positive terminal for the galvanic cell. In an electrolytic cell, where electrons are provided by an external current, anode acquires a positive charge whereas the cathode is negatively charged.

options.text: None of these options are correct

questions.explanation: Oxidation occurs at the anode and reduction occurs at the cathode. Since the reaction at the anode is the source of electrons for the current, the anode is the negative terminal for the galvanic cell.

questions.text: Which increases down the group for alkali metals?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The reactivity of Group I elements increases as you go down the group because: the atoms get larger as you go down the group, the outer electron goes farther away from the nucleus and can thus be easily removed. Due to the same reason, the electron affinity and ionization energy of group I elements decreases as we go down the group.

options.text: All of the above options are correct

questions.explanation: The reactivity of Group I elements increases as you go down the group because: the atoms get larger as you go down the group. the outer electron gets further from the nucleus as you go down the group.

questions.text: Electron affinity decreases down the group due to:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Electron affinity decreases from top to bottom within a group. This is caused by the increase in atomic radius, as they move farther away from the nucleus , it becomes relatively easier to remove them and the nuclear attraction also decreases.

options.text: All of the above options are correct

questions.explanation: Electron affinity decreases from top to bottom within a group. This is caused by the increase in atomic radius.

questions.text: It is impossible for two electrons to have same value of spin quantum number in the same orbital, according to:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The Pauli Exclusion Principle states that, in an atom or molecule, no two electrons can have the same four electronic quantum numbers, at max two electrons can have three same quantum numbers and one different. As an orbital can contain a maximum of only two electrons, the two electrons must have opposing spins.

options.text: All of the above options are correct

questions.explanation: The Pauli Exclusion Principle states that, in an atom or molecule, no two electrons can have the same four electronic quantum numbers. As an orbital can contain a maximum of only two electrons, the two electrons must have opposing spins.

questions.text: Which one is least ionic?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The ionic characteristics decrease as we move from left to right in the periodic table and it increases as we move top to bottom in any group. In period 3, out of the given options Na is at left, Mg in middle and Al at the right.

questions.explanation: The ionic characteristics decrease as we move from left to right in the periodic table and it increases as we move top to bottom in any group.

questions.text: Diamond is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Diamond is a network covalent solid. In a diamond Carbon is covalently bonded into a network like structure.

questions.explanation: Diamond is a network covalent solid.

questions.text: Ideal gas equation is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: According to the Ideal Gas equation: The product of Pressure & Volume of a gas bears a constant relation with the product of Universal gas constant, number of moles of gas and temperature. ie. PV = nRT.

questions.text: How many moles of CO2 contain 16g of oxygen?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: One molecule of CO2 contains 2 oxygen atoms, hence one mole of CO2 will contain 32 g of oxygen. 0.5 moles of CO2 contains 16 g of oxygen.

questions.explanation: 1 mol of CO2 contains = 32 g O2=1 6 g O2 will be present in = 16/32 = 0.5 mol

questions.text: Stoichiometry is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Relationship between quantities of substances in a chemical reaction
options.explanation: By definition the relationship between the study of relative quantities of substances taking part in a reaction or forming a compound is called stoichiometry.

options.text: Relationship between amount of substances in a chemical equation
All of the above options are correct

questions.explanation: The relationship between the relative quantities of substances taking part in a reaction or forming a compound is called stoichiometry.

questions.text: What is the most common gas in the environment?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The most abundant naturally occurring gas is nitrogen (N2), which makes up about 78% of air. Oxygen (O2) is the second most abundant gas at about 21%. The inert gas argon (Ar) is the third most abundant gas at 0.93%.

questions.explanation: The most abundant naturally occurring gas is nitrogen (N2), which makes up about 78% of air. Oxygen (O2) is the second most abundant gas at about 21%. The inert gas argon (Ar) is the third most abundant gas at 0.93%.

questions.text: Proton is how many times heavier than an electron?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: A proton is 1836 times heavier than an electron.

questions.explanation: Protons are 1836 times heavier than electrons.

questions.text: Which quantum number tells shape of the orbital?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The Azimuthal quantum number (l) describes the shape of the orbital.

questions.explanation: The Azimuthal quantum number (l) describes the shape of the orbital.

questions.text: The number of electrons in the p subshell is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The p subshell has 3 orbitals px, py and pz. Each orbital can at max hold 2 electrons, hence, p subshell can hold a maximum of 6 electrons.

questions.explanation: p subshell has 3 orbit and 1 orbit has maximum 2 electrons.

questions.text: Which one of the following has greater bond energy?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: HF has greater bond energy than HCl. The bond energy of HF is 568 kJ/mol, while the bond energy of HCl is 431 kJ/mol. HF has the greatest bond energy because F is the most electronegative atom and pulls electrons to the greatest extent. The trend of bond energy in hydrohalides is as follows; HF > HCl > HBr > HI

questions.explanation: HF has greater bond energy than HCl. The bond energy of HF is 568 kJ/moL, while the bond energy of HCl is 431 kJ/moL.

questions.text: Oxidation number of an element in free state is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Free state means uncombined state, i.e., atomic state. In an uncombined state the oxidation number of an element will always be zero.

questions.explanation: Free state means uncombined state, i.e., atomic state. In an uncombined state the oxidation number will always be zero.

questions.text: Which of the following is light as a feather and strong as iron?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: It is as light as a feather, more than 100 times stronger than steel and a very good conductor of electricity.

questions.explanation: It is as light as a feather, more than 100 times stronger than steel and a lightning conductor of electricity.

questions.text: The maximum density of water is at:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The maximum density of water is at precisely 3.98oC or approximately 4oC. At lower or higher temperature, the density of water increases. The density of ice is less than liquid water, so it floats. Upon freezing, the ice density decreases by about 9%.

questions.explanation: The maximum density of water is at 4°C as there are two opposite effects that are in balance. The density of ice is less than liquid water, so it floats. Upon freezing, the ice density decreases by about 9%.

questions.text: Homocyclic hydrocarbons are further divided into:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Homocyclic compounds are divided into two compounds. Alicyclic compounds and aromatic compounds.

questions.explanation: Homocyclic compounds are divided into two compounds. Alicyclic compounds and aromatic compounds.

questions.text: Which contains sp2 hybridization?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: None of these options are correct
options.explanation: The carbon atoms of methane and ethane are sp3 hybridized and that of alkynes are sp hybridized.

questions.explanation: The hybridization of methane molecules occurs by mixing one orbital with three p orbitals. In ethane (CH3CH3), both carbons are sp3-hybridised. Alkynes undergo sp hybridization.

questions.text: Formula of wood spirit is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Methanol (CH3OH) is also referred as wood alcohol or wood spirit or wood naphtha as the earliest method for its preparation was by destructive distillation of wood.

questions.explanation: Methanol is also referred as wood alcohol or wood spirit or wood naphtha as the earliest method for its preparation was by destructive distillation of wood.

questions.text: Geometric isomerism is shown by:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Geometric Isomerism or cis-trans isomerism results most commonly from C=C. The important property which introduces the feature is the inability of the Carbon atoms to rotate relative to one another about the double bond. It is not possible in alkanes due to rotation around bond. It is not possible in alkynes due to only one group attached with each of the triple bonded atom.

options.text: All of the above options are correct

questions.explanation: Geometric Isomerism results most commonly from C=C. The important property which introduces the feature is the inability of the Carbon atoms to rotate relative to one another about the double bond.

questions.text: CnH2nO2 is the general formula of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The general formula CnH2nO2 is for carboxylic acids and esters. The general formula of alcohols and ethers is Cn H2n+1 OH. The general formula of ketones is CnH2nO.

questions.explanation: The general formula CnH2nO2 is for carboxylic acids and esters.

questions.text: Alcohols and phenols can be differentiated by which reaction?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Phenols react with bromine water to give a white ppt. of 2,4,6-tribromophenol. Alcohols do not have any action with bromine water. Option D cannot be correct because not all alcohols give Iodoform test. It is limited to secondary alcohols and ethanol only.

questions.explanation: Phenols react with bromine water to give a white ppt. of 2,4,6-tribromophenol. Alcohols do not have any action with bromine water. Option D cannot be correct because not all alcohols give Iodoform test. It is limited to secondary alcohols and ethanol only.

questions.text: 50% NaOH is used in:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: In accordance with Punjab Textbook Board page 237, 50% concentrated NaOH solution is generally used in Cannizaro reaction as it is used for aldehydes which do not have α-H.

questions.text: Carboxylic acids are soluble in water due to:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Hydrogen bonds are formed between the individual molecules of the acid and water molecules. It is because of these interactions that carboxylic acids can dissolve in water to form acidic solutions.

questions.explanation: Hydrogen bonds are formed between the individual molecules of the acid and water molecules. It is because of these interactions that carboxylic acids can dissolve in water to form acidic solutions.

questions.text: Esterification occurs in the presence of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Esterification is the process of combining an organic acid (RCOOH) with an alcohol (ROH) to form an ester (RCOOR) and water; or a chemical reaction resulting in the formation of at least one ester product. Ester is obtained by an esterification reaction of an alcohol and a carboxylic acid.

questions.explanation: Esterification is the process of combining an organic acid (RCOOH) with an alcohol (ROH) to form an ester (RCOOR) and water; or a chemical reaction resulting in the formation of at least one ester product. Ester is obtained by an esterification reaction of an alcohol and a carboxylic acid.

questions.text: Benzene can be prepared from sodium benzoate by reaction with:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Sodium benzoate is converted to benzene by heating with a mixture of NaOH and CaO.

questions.explanation: Sodium benzoate is converted to benzene by heating with a mixture of NaOH and CaO.

questions.text: Empirical formula is related to molecular formula by:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The general relation between both the empirical and molecular formula is, (Molecular Formula = n × Empirical Formula). This can be used for finding both the empirical formula and the molecular m\formula of the compound. n in the relation represents the ratio of molecular mass and empirical mass of the compound. Hence, this option is incorrect.

The molecular formula is a representation of a compound that shows the actual number of atoms of each element present in a molecule. It can be the same as or a multiple of the empirical formula. The empirical formula, on the other hand, represents the simplest whole-number ratio of the atoms of each element in a compound. It does not provide the exact number of atoms but gives the relative proportions. The relationship between the molecular formula and the empirical formula is given by the formula: Molecular formula = (Empirical formula) × n
The relationship between the molecular formula and the empirical formula is given by the formula: Molecular formula = (Empirical formula) × n Hence this option is incorrect.

The relationship between the molecular formula and the empirical formula is given by the formula: Molecular formula = (Empirical formula) × n The positioning of empirical formula is wrong in this option.

questions.explanation: Empirical formulas show the simplest whole-number ratio of atoms in a compound, molecular formulas show the number of each type of atom in a molecule.

questions.text: Which one is more reactive among halogens?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The reactivity of halogens decreases with increase in atomic number of halogens. So, the correct order of reactivity of halogens is; F2 > Cl2 > Br2 > I2

questions.explanation: Order of reactivity towards halogenation is F2 > Cl2 >Br2 > I2.

questions.text: One calorie is equal to:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: 1 cal (calorie) equals 4.18 J (joules).

questions.explanation: One calorie (cal) equals 4.18 J. (Fact)

questions.text: The net enthalpy change in a chemical reaction is same whether it is brought about in two or more different ways in one or several steps. It is known as:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: According to Hess's law, the thermal effects of a reaction depend upon the initial and final conditions of the reacting substances. It does not depend upon the intermediate steps.

questions.explanation: According to Hess's law, the thermal effects of a reaction depends upon the initial and final conditions of the reacting substances. It does not depend upon the immediate steps.

questions.text: Which is not true about rate of reaction?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: It is independent of concentration of reactants.
options.explanation: Option a) "It is independent of the concentration of reactants" is not true about the rate of reaction. The rate of a chemical reaction is typically dependent on the concentration of reactants. In fact, one of the fundamental principles of chemical kinetics is the relationship between the rate of reaction and the concentrations of reactants, often expressed in the form of rate laws.

"It depends upon the temperature": This is true. The rate of a chemical reaction is strongly influenced by temperature. Generally, as temperature increases, the rate of reaction increases due to the higher kinetic energy of molecules, which leads to more frequent and energetic collisions between reactant molecules.

options.text: It does not depend upon the order of reaction.
options.explanation: "It does not depend upon the order of reaction": This is true. The rate of a reaction does not depend on the order of the reaction. The order of reaction determines how the rate depends on the concentration of reactants but does not affect the fact that the rate depends on concentration.

options.text: All of the above options are correct.
options.explanation: "All of the above options are correct": This statement is not correct because option a) is not true, as explained above.

questions.explanation: Option a) "It is independent of the concentration of reactants" is not true about the rate of reaction. The rate of a chemical reaction is typically dependent on the concentration of reactants. In fact, one of the fundamental principles of chemical kinetics is the relationship between the rate of reaction and the concentrations of reactants, often expressed in the form of rate laws.

questions.text: Which one is a s block element?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: s-block comprises 14 elements namely hydrogen (H), lithium (Li), helium (He), sodium (Na), beryllium (Be), potassium (K), magnesium (Mg), rubidium (Rb). B and Al belong to the p-block and Cu belongs to d-block elements.

questions.explanation: s-block comprises 14 elements namely hydrogen (H), lithium (Li), helium (He), sodium (Na), beryllium (Be), potassium (K), magnesium (Mg), rubidium (Rb).

questions.text: Water is a universal solvent due to:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The water can form 4 hydrogen bonds as the intermolecular forces of interaction with other molecules. It can perform multiple hydrogen bonds that will result to higher interaction with other molecules. An increase in the interaction of the solvent with the solute will result in an increase in solubility. This makes water a universal solvent since it can dissolve many solutes due to the capability to form multiple hydrogen bonds. It is also partially polar in nature.

questions.explanation: The water can form 4 hydrogen bonds as the intermolecular forces of interaction with other molecules. It can perform multiple hydrogen bonds that will result to higher interaction with other molecules. An increase in the interaction of the solvent with the solute will result in an increase in solubility. This makes water a universal solvent since it can dissolve many solutes due to the capability to form multiple hydrogen bonds.

questions.text: Bond angle is 120˚ in:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: C2H4 is a planar molecule and each bond angle is about 120 degrees.

questions.explanation: C2H4 is a planar molecule and each bond angle is about 120 degrees.

questions.text: The time rate of change of velocity is called:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Acceleration is defined as the rate of change of velocity with respect to time. Acceleration is a vector quantity, so it includes both magnitude and direction. Mathematically; a = Δv/ Δt

questions.explanation: Acceleration is defined as the rate of change of velocity with respect to time. Acceleration is a vector quantity, so it includes both magnitude and direction.

questions.text: In projectile motion, the range of projectile will be maximum at an angle of:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The range of projectile is given by; R = v2sin2θ / g When θ = 45o Then, sin2θ = sin(2x45) = sin90 = 1 So, we get maximum value of R; Rmax = v2 / g

questions.explanation: The greatest the value of sin of anything can be is 1. This angle will be at 45 degrees.

questions.text: Which of the following is not true?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Momentum is not a vector quantity
options.explanation: Momentum is a vector quantity. The direction of an object's momentum vector is in the direction that the object is moving. Mathematically; P = mv

options.text: Momentum is product of mass and velocity

questions.explanation: Momentum is a vector quantity. The direction of an object's momentum vector is in the direction that the object is moving.

questions.text: In perfect elastic collision
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Momentum, kinetic energy, and total energy is conserved
options.explanation: Momentum and total energy are conserved in both elastic and inelastic motion. Kinetic energy is conserved in elastic motion but not conserved in inelastic motion.

questions.explanation: Kinetic energy and hence momentum is conserved only in elastic collision. The kinetic energy is not conserved in inelastic collisions but the total energy is conserved in all types of collisions.

questions.text: The slope of displacement-time graph is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The slope of the displacement-time graph gives the velocity of an object while the slope of the distance-time graph gives the speed of an object. Mathematically, slope = Δy / Δx Slope = velocity = Δdisplacement / Δtime

questions.explanation: The slope of the displacement-time graph gives the velocity of an object while the slope of the distance-time graph gives the speed of an object.

questions.text: Two stones, 10kg and 50kg fall through 80m high cliff. Which stone has greater velocity at bottom? (ignoring air resistance) (g=10ms-2)
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: It was observed by Galileo that if we neglect air resistance all objects reach the ground at the same time so their velocity will be the same.

questions.explanation: If we neglect air resistance both objects reach the ground at the same time so their velocity will be the same.

questions.text: How much work is done by force of gravity in pulling a stone of weight 10N from the top of 250m high cliff to the foot of cliff?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: W = F . d It can also be written as; W = w . h W = 10 x 250 W = 2500 J

questions.explanation: Potential energy = Mgh Mg (weight) is given as 10N So, 250x10=2500.

questions.text: When the speed of your car is halved, by what factor does its kinetic energy decrease?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: As, KE = ½ mv2 KE/ = ½ mv/2 Given that; v/ = v/2 KE/ = ½ m(v/2)2 KE/ = ¼ (½ mv2) KE/ = ¼ KE

questions.explanation: If the speed is halved (keeping the mass same), the kinetic energy decreases, it becomes one-fourth (since kinetic energy is proportional to the square of velocity).

questions.text: If by some phenomenon the Earth contracts to one half of its present size, then the value of gravitational potential energy becomes:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The formula for gravitational potential energy is U = -GMm/r Hence, U ∝ 1/r. If r/ = r/2 then U/ ∝ 1/(r/2). Therefore U ∝ 2(1/r), Hence, the gravitational potential energy is doubled.

questions.explanation: The formula for gravitational potential energy is U = -GMm/r Hence, U = 1/r. If r/2, then U = 1/(r/2). Therefore U = 2(1/r), the gravitational potential energy is doubled.

questions.text: The product of force and velocity is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: P = W / t P = Fd / t P = F (d/t) P = F v Hence, power is the product of force and velocity.

questions.explanation: The product of force and velocity is called power.

questions.text: One degree is equal to:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: 2π rad = 360 degrees π rad = 180 degrees 1 degree = π/180 = 0.0175 rad

questions.explanation: 2π rad = 360 degrees 1 degree = 2π/360 = 0.0175

questions.text: An object moving along a circular path of radius 1m. It covers a distance of 2m along the circumference of the circle. What will be the angular displacement covered by it?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Angular Displacement = s/r s=2 and r=1 so, angular displacement = 2/1 = 2 rad

questions.explanation: Angular Displacement =s/r s=2 and r=1 so angular displacement = 2

questions.text: If we double, both the speed of the body and radius of the circular path, the centripetal force will be:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: F = mv2 / r If we double both radius and velocity then; F/ = m (2v)2 / 2r F/ = 2 (mv2/r) F/ = 2 F Hence, by doubling both the radius and the velocity, the centripetal force is doubled.

questions.explanation: If both the speed of a body and radius of its circular path are doubled, then centripetal force also gets doubled.

questions.text: Relationship between angular and linear velocity is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: v = rω is the relation between linear and angular velocity.

questions.explanation: Relation Between Linear Velocity and Angular Velocity |v| = rω, which is the relation between linear and angular velocity.

questions.text: In a ripple tank 100 waves pass through a certain point in one second. If the wavelength of the wave is 1cm then the speed of the wave will be?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Frequency=100 Hz Wavelength= 1cm = 1/100 = 0.01m. Speed = (Frequency) (Wavelength) = (100) (0.01). = 1m/s.

questions.explanation: Frequency=100, Wavelength= 1cm = 1/100 = 0.01m. Frequency = Speed/Wavelength (Frequency) (Wavelength) =Speed (100)(0.01)=1m/s.

questions.text: To make frequency of spring oscillation four times, we have to:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: f = 1/2π (√k/m) or, f ∝ 1/ √m If we reduce the mass by 16 times then; f/ ∝ 1/ √(m/16) f/ ∝ 4 (1/ √m) f/ ∝ 4 f

questions.explanation: Frequency of the spring oscillator is directly protion to √(1/m). Thus to quadraple the frequency, we will have to reduce the mass by 1/16

questions.text: Speed of sound is 332ms-1 at 0˚C. What will be its value at 10˚C?
questions.subject: Physics

questions.text: In molar specific heat, the equation Cp-Cv = R shows that:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: As, Cp - Cv = R Cp = Cv + R Hence, Cp is equal to the sum of Cv and R and as R is always positive, this implies that Cp > Cv

questions.explanation: So, Cp is greater than Cv, and Cp - Cv = R is a representation of this relationship for ideal gases. This means that for a given amount of substance, when heat is added at constant pressure, the temperature change is greater than when heat is added at constant volume.

questions.text: The thermodynamic process during which volume of the system remains constant is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: In thermodynamics, an isochoric process, also called a constant-volume process, an isovolumetric process, or an isometric process, is a thermodynamic process during which the volume of the closed system undergoing such a process remains constant.

questions.explanation: In thermodynamics, an isochoric process, also called a constant-volume process, an isovolumetric process, or an isometric process, is a thermodynamic process during which the volume of the closed system undergoing such a process remains constant.

questions.text: If two capacitors of capacitance 2 μF and 6 μF are connected in parallel, the equivalent capacitance of combination will be:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: If two capacitors of capacitance 2 μF and 6 μF are connected in parallel, the equivalent capacitance of combination will be the direct sum. Ceq = C1 + C2 Ceq = 2 + 6 Ceq = 8 μF

questions.explanation: If two capacitors of capacitance 2 μF and 6 μF are connected in parallel, the equivalent capacitance of combination will be the direct sum.

questions.text: Capacitance of a capacitor does not depend upon:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The capacitance is given by C = ϵ A / d Hence, it depends on distance between plates (d), area of plates (A) and the medium (ϵ), but it does not depend on the nature of metal used.

questions.explanation: Capacitance of a capacitor does not depend upon metal of plates.

questions.text: Power transfer will be maximum when:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Maximum Power Transfer occurs when the resistive value of the load is equal in value to that of the voltage source's internal resistance.

questions.explanation: Maximum Power Transfer occurs when the resistive value of the load is equal in value to that of the voltage source's internal resistance.

questions.text: A wire is stretched so that its radius of cross section becomes half, then resistance of wire will be:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: R = ρ l / A When we stretch the wire to decrease its radius to half the area decreases by 4 times and to keep the volume constant the length increases by 4 times, so the new resistance becomes; R/ = ρ (4l) / (A/4) R/ = 16 (ρ l / A) R/ = 16 R Hence, the resistance increases by 16 times.

questions.explanation: If you stretch the wire till its radius is halved the previous value, then its length will increase 4 times to keep the volume constant. Resistivity remains the same as it depends upon the material of the wire. So, the new resistance will be R = 16R.

questions.text: When a charged particle enters the magnetic field parallel, it will:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: F = q v B sinθ Where sinθ = sin0 = 0 F = 0 If the particle is moving along the magnetic field, F=0. Hence the particle continues to move along the incident direction, in a straight line.

questions.explanation: If the particle is moving along the magnetic field, F=0. Hence the particle continues to move along the incident direction, in a straight line.

questions.text: Magnetic flux is maximum when angle between magnetic field and vector area is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Magnetic flux is maximum when the angle between the magnetic field and the normal to the plane of finite area is 0 degree. As, flux = BAcos θ So, when θ = 0 Then, cosθ = cos0 = 1 (maximum)

questions.explanation: Magnetic flux is maximum when the angle between the magnetic field and the normal to the plane of finite area is 0 degree. As flux = BAcos θ, so when θ is 0 then cosθ will be 1 (maximum).

questions.text: The domestic electricity supply in Pakistan has a frequency of:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Pakistan operates on a 230V supply voltage and a frequency of 50Hz.

questions.explanation: Pakistan operates on a 230V supply voltage and 50Hz.

questions.text: The device which increases or decreases the emf is called:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A transformer is a device which increases or decreases alternating voltage through mutual induction. It consists of two coils wound on the same code. Its working formula is; Vs/Vp = ns/np = Ip/Is

questions.explanation: A transformer is a device which increases or decreases alternating voltage through mutual induction. It consists of two coils wound on the same code.

questions.text: The induced current flow in such a direction so as to oppose the cause that produces it. The statement is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Lenz's law, in electromagnetism, states that an induced electric current flows in a direction such that the current opposes the change that induced it.

questions.explanation: Lenz's law, in electromagnetism, states that an induced electric current flows in a direction such that the current opposes the change that induced it.

questions.text: The device which is used for conversion of AC into DC is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The device that is used to convert AC to DC is a rectifier. A rectifier corrects or straightens the direction of the current. This device periodically reverses the direction of alternating current (AC) to direct current (DC), which usually flows in just one direction.

questions.explanation: The device that is used to convert AC to DC is a rectifier. A rectifier corrects or straightens the direction of the current. This device periodically reverses the direction of alternating current (AC) to direct current (DC), which usually flows in just one direction.

questions.text: The process of conversion of AC into DC is called:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The process of converting AC to DC is called rectification. The device used for rectification is called the rectifier.

questions.explanation: The process of converting AC to DC is called rectification. The device used for rectification is called the rectifier.

questions.text: In projectile motion, at maximum height:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: At the highest point of projectile motion, the vertical velocity is equal to 0 (Vy = 0) and horizontal velocity will be constant and it remains constant throughout the motion (vx = constant).

questions.explanation: At the highest point of projectile motion, the vertical velocity is equal to 0 (Vy = 0) and horizontal velocity will be constant (vx = constant).

questions.text: A 32g phosphorus is decayed and 2g remains undecayed after 60 days. The half-life of phosphorous is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Initially = 32 g 1 half-life = 16g 2 half-lives = 8g 3 half-lives = 4g 4 half-lives = 2g Hence, a total of 4 half-lives have passed in a period of 60 days, so; Half life = Total time / no of half-lives passed 60/4=15 days

questions.explanation: 1 half life = 16g 2 half life = 8g 3 half life = 4g 4 half life = 2g 60/4=15 days

questions.text: The SI unit of gravitational potential is :
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The amount of work required to transport a body of unit mass from infinity to some point is known as gravitational potential. The mathematical expression will be G = W/m. The unit of work is joule and the unit of mass is the kilogram (kg), so the unit of gravitation potential will be J/Kg.

questions.explanation: The amount of work required to transport a body of unit mass from infinity to some point is known as gravitational potential. The mathematical expression will be G = W/m. The unit of work is joule and the unit of mass is the kilogram (kg), so the unit of gravitation potential will be J/Kg.

questions.text: In nuclear radiography for medical diagnosis which rays are used?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Diagnostic techniques in nuclear medicine use radioactive tracers which emit gamma rays from within the body.

questions.explanation: Diagnostic techniques in nuclear medicine use radioactive tracers which emit gamma rays from within the body.

questions.text: Unit of linear momentum is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: As, P = F/t = N/s. So N/s is a unit of momentum.

questions.explanation: The unit for momentum can be Newton-second (Ns).

questions.text: General gas equation formula:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Gas equation is PV = nRT.
